{"id":"project-LICHEN-p26q","title":"handle_icmpv6 responds to invalid source addresses","description":"File: python/src/lichen/ipv6/icmpv6.py:230-265

The handle_icmpv6() function does not validate the source address of incoming packets before sending a reply.

Per RFC 4443 section 2.4(e), ICMPv6 messages MUST NOT be sent in response to packets with certain source addresses:
- The unspecified address (::)
- Any multicast address

Current code (lines 256-257):
```python
reply_src = packet.header.dst_addr
reply_dst = packet.header.src_addr
```

If the Echo Request has src_addr=:: (unspecified) or src_addr=ff02::1 (multicast), the reply would be sent to that invalid address.

Impact:
- Replying to :: could cause packets to be dropped or misrouted
- Replying to a multicast address violates protocol and could cause amplification attacks

Fix: Before generating a reply, check:
```python
if packet.header.src_addr.is_unspecified or packet.header.src_addr.is_multicast:
    return None  # RFC 4443: do not reply
```

Tags: protocol, security, ipv6","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:22:15.287713Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:22:15.287752Z"}
